# stream-csv-as-json

> Micro-library of Node.js stream components for creating custom CSV processing pipelines with a minimal memory footprint. Parse CSV files far exceeding available memory using a SAX-inspired streaming token API. Companion to `stream-json`; uses the same token protocol. One dependency: `stream-json`.

## Install

npm i stream-csv-as-json

## Quick start

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');
const fs = require('fs');

const pipeline = chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => console.log(value)
]);
```

## API

### Parser

`Parser` — streaming CSV parser producing `{name, value}` tokens. Extends `Transform`.

- `new Parser(options)` / `Parser.make(options)` / `Parser.parser(options)` — create instance.
- Options: `packStrings`/`packValues` (default: true), `streamStrings`/`streamValues` (default: true), `separator` (default: `','`).

```js
const Parser = require('stream-csv-as-json/Parser');
const p = new Parser();
fs.createReadStream('data.csv').pipe(p);
p.on('data', token => console.log(token.name, token.value));
```

### Main module

The default export creates a Parser with `emit()` applied:

```js
const make = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const stream = make();
stream.on('startArray', () => { /* row start */ });
stream.on('stringValue', val => { /* field value */ });
```

### AsObjects

`AsObjects` — Transform stream that uses the first CSV row as field names and converts subsequent rows from array tokens to object tokens.

- `AsObjects.make(options)` / `AsObjects.asObjects(options)` — create instance.
- `AsObjects.withParser(options)` — create pipeline with CSV parser.
- Options: `packKeys` (default: true), `streamKeys` (default: true), `useStringValues`/`useValues` (default: false), `fieldPrefix` (default: `'field'`).

```js
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');

chain([parser(), asObjects(), streamValues(), ({value}) => console.log(value)]);
```

### Stringer

`Stringer` — Transform stream converting CSV token stream back to CSV text.

- `Stringer.make(options)` / `Stringer.stringer(options)` — create instance.
- Options: `useStringValues`/`useValues` (default: false), `separator` (default: `','`).

```js
const Stringer = require('stream-csv-as-json/Stringer');
chain([parser(), Stringer.make(), fs.createWriteStream('output.csv')]);
```

## Token protocol

The parser emits `{name, value}` tokens: `startArray`, `endArray`, `startString`, `endString`, `stringChunk`, `stringValue`. After `AsObjects`: `startObject`, `endObject`, `startKey`, `endKey`, `keyValue`.

## Common patterns

### Stream a huge CSV as objects

```js
const {chain} = require('stream-chain');
const {parser} = require('stream-csv-as-json');
const {asObjects} = require('stream-csv-as-json/AsObjects');
const {streamValues} = require('stream-json/streamers/StreamValues');

chain([
  fs.createReadStream('huge.csv'),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => processRow(value)
]);
```

### Compressed CSV processing

```js
const zlib = require('zlib');

chain([
  fs.createReadStream('data.csv.gz'),
  zlib.createGunzip(),
  parser(),
  asObjects(),
  streamValues(),
  ({value}) => value.department === 'accounting' ? value : null
]);
```

### CSV round-trip

```js
const Stringer = require('stream-csv-as-json/Stringer');

chain([
  fs.createReadStream('input.csv'),
  parser(),
  Stringer.make(),
  fs.createWriteStream('output.csv')
]);
```

## Links

- Docs: https://github.com/uhop/stream-csv-as-json/wiki
- npm: https://www.npmjs.com/package/stream-csv-as-json
- Full LLM reference: https://github.com/uhop/stream-csv-as-json/blob/master/llms-full.txt
